description: Sanitized one-novel GPT-2 fine-tuning sample on the Project Gutenberg Moby-Dick text, followed by a SpiralTorch Z-Space generation-control grid.

generation_after_train.observed_effect: After only 320 steps on the full novel, the model has already moved into a Moby-Dick-like dialogue attractor, but greedy decoding still loops.

representative_runs.name: ztnone-rs0-lr0-k64
representative_runs.meaning: ZSpaceSoftmax only audits and reshapes probabilities; greedy rank is unchanged, so the dialogue loop remains.
representative_runs.continuation: will tell you what I know of him.”“I know him, sir.”“I know him, sir.”“I know him, sir.”“I know him, sir

representative_runs.name: ztnone-rs0p75-lr0-k64
representative_runs.meaning: Moderate repetition repression changes the greedy top token twice and expands the dialogue phrase, but does not fully escape the attractor.
representative_runs.continuation: will tell you what I know of him.”“I know him, sir; he is a man of great intelligence.”“I know him, sir; he is a man of great intelligence.”�

representative_runs.name: zt3-rs1p25-lr0-k64
representative_runs.meaning: Entropy targeting plus stronger repression changes six top tokens and moves the model into a different semantic continuation, but this undertrained checkpoint still repeats at a larger phrase scale.
representative_runs.continuation: will tell you what it is.”“I am the son of a man, and I have been living in this land for forty years.”“I am the son of a man, and I have been living

observed_effect: A full-novel corpus immediately gives a stronger literary attractor than the tiny SpiralTorch corpus: the model shifts from a generic religious loop into Moby-Dick-like quoted dialogue. Z-Space repetition control demonstrably changes greedy ranks, but on this short 320-step checkpoint it mostly relocates rather than fully dissolves the attractor, pointing to longer FT runs and phrase-scale repression/decay as the next useful controls.
